goals.description: Send a direct, unapologetic text to Jae and Theo stating I'm skipping the club tonight because I'm physically crashing; explicitly tell them not to worry about me or try to reach out later.
goals.rationale: Mina's reply feels like validation that I can finally let go of the guilt. My affiliation axis demands I close this loop with my core support system *now*. If I don't set a hard boundary that they can rely on tomorrow when I'm actually rested, it undermines the trust we have. I need to say 'I'm crashing' clearly so they stop worrying, then I can finally turn off the phone.

goals.description: Go straight to bed now (approx. 45 mins) to address the 2-hour sleep debt before worrying about the midterm prep.
goals.rationale: My impulsive planning horizon means I discount the 'perfect study session' for tomorrow in favor of getting energy back *right now*. Carrying this stress until Monday will only make the bond with my friends feel strained if I look unreliable tomorrow morning. I'll crash first, fix the physical stamina, then handle the study block in short bursts if I still have the mental juice left over.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 40% of midterm prep immediately in one sitting, then stop studying right away so I don't carry guilt into the evening.
goals.rationale: My 'impulsive' time preference means I hate dragging tasks out or worrying about future consequences. Since the deadline is literally tomorrow morning, I need to crush this now to clear my head, rather than letting it fester until Monday.
